proposed_hypotheses.text: The cohort contains 50000 patients with a single continuous outcome pfs_months whose distribution is right-skewed with a mean near 4 months; the 33 anonymous features include binary indicators (likely treatments / disease features), a 3-level ordinal (likely performance status), and continuous lab-like values.

analyses.result_summary: n=50000, 33 features. pfs_months: mean 4.31, median 4.21, sd 2.29, max 16.25. Binary features include feature_031 (44.7%), feature_010 (44.8%), feature_014 (55%), feature_006 (35%), feature_023 (42%), feature_024 (30%), feature_027 (20%), feature_012 (15%), feature_018 (10%), feature_020 (8%) and rare flags feature_011/022/016/028/002 (<5%). feature_001 takes values 0/1/2 with prevalences 35/50/15%. Continuous features span lab-like ranges (e.g. feature_015 mean 65 sd 10; feature_019 mean 3.8 sd 0.5; feature_021 mean 224 sd 81; feature_005 mean 9.2 sd 16 max 777).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Several features are strongly and significantly associated with pfs_months; in particular feature_015 is positively correlated with pfs_months while feature_001, feature_014, feature_006, feature_023, feature_005, feature_022, feature_025 are negatively associated and feature_027, feature_019, feature_011 are positively associated.

analyses.code: for col in features: stats.pearsonr or stats.ttest_ind on pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: feature_015 (Pearson r=+0.766, p<1e-300) far exceeds any other feature's univariate effect. Strongly negative: feature_001 (mean PFS 5.27 -> 4.05 -> 2.91 across 0/1/2), feature_014 (5.06 vs 3.70, p<1e-300), feature_023 (4.45 vs 4.12, p=8.5e-56), feature_006 (4.42 vs 4.11, p=5.2e-46), feature_005 (r=-0.038), feature_022 (4.32 vs 4.09, p=3.7e-6), feature_025 (r=-0.123). Strongly positive: feature_027 (mean PFS 5.09 vs 4.12, p<1e-300), feature_019 (r=+0.10), feature_011 (4.53 vs 4.31, p=2.3e-4). Marginal: feature_021 r=-0.011 p=0.013, feature_030 r=-0.013 p=0.004, feature_013 r=-0.009 p=0.044. Null overall: feature_010 (4.32 vs 4.30 p=0.35), feature_031 (4.31 vs 4.31 p=0.77), feature_018 (p=0.87), feature_012 (p=0.86), feature_002, feature_007, feature_009, feature_017, feature_029, feature_033 etc.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a fully-adjusted multivariable linear regression of pfs_months on all 33 features, the strongest independent prognostic effects are: feature_015 (positive, ~+0.18 months per unit), feature_014 (negative, -1.36), feature_001 (negative ordinal, -1.17 per level), feature_027 (positive, +0.94), feature_019 (positive, +0.47), feature_023 (-0.35), feature_022 (-0.36), feature_006 (-0.29), feature_025 (-0.08 per unit), feature_005 (-0.005 per unit), feature_021 (-0.0004 per unit); features 010, 031, 018, 012, 020, 024, 002, 003, 004, 007, 008, 009, 011, 013, 016, 017, 026, 028, 029, 030, 032, 033 do not retain a significant adjusted main effect.

analyses.result_summary: OLS R^2=0.859 (n=50000). Adjusted coefficients (t, p): feature_015 +0.176 (t=453.6), feature_001 -1.171 (t=-205.7), feature_014 -1.362 (t=-175.6), feature_027 +0.943 (t=97.9), feature_019 +0.469 (t=61.0), feature_022 -0.357 (t=-18.9), feature_023 -0.354 (t=-43.9), feature_006 -0.287 (t=-35.5), feature_025 -0.076 (t=-76.1), feature_005 -0.0050 (t=-21.0), feature_021 -0.00043 (t=-8.9). All others |t|<2 or p>0.05. The huge t-statistic for feature_015 (and r=0.77 in univariate) implies it explains a large share of pfs_months variance on its own.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 (binary, 20% prevalence, +0.94 month adjusted main effect) is the leading candidate for a treatment indicator. feature_031 (45%) and feature_010 (45%) are balanced binaries with essentially zero main effect on pfs_months and are secondary candidates whose effects (if any) might be confined to subgroups.

analyses.code: compare prevalence and main effects of binary features
analyses.result_summary: feature_027 prevalence 0.200, marginal PFS difference +0.972 months (5.089 vs 4.117). feature_031 prevalence 0.447, marginal PFS difference -0.006 months (n.s.). feature_010 prevalence 0.448, marginal PFS difference -0.019 months (n.s.). The combination 'balanced + null marginal' for feature_031 and feature_010 is consistent with either no effect or a treatment with offsetting heterogeneous effects across subgroups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_027 on pfs_months is concentrated in patients with feature_023=0 and is essentially absent in patients with feature_023=1 (treatment-by-feature_023 interaction).
Independently of feature_023, the benefit of feature_027 is concentrated in patients with feature_006=0 and absent in patients with feature_006=1.
Independently of feature_023 and feature_006, the benefit of feature_027 is restricted to patients with low feature_005 and disappears at higher feature_005 values.
Independently of the other modifiers, the benefit of feature_027 is restricted to patients with feature_022=0 and is absent or reversed when feature_022=1.

analyses.code: for v in features: sm.OLS(pfs_months ~ feature_027 + v + feature_027:v).fit() and rank by interaction p
analyses.result_summary: Treatment-by-feature interaction screen for feature_027 vs every other feature. Strongest interactions (interaction coefficient on pfs_months, p): feature_005>median -1.67 (p=4e-244), feature_023 -1.63 (p=8e-226), feature_006 -1.45 (p=4e-168), feature_022 -1.18 (p=6e-23). Each modifier roughly cancels the +1-2 month treatment main effect, indicating each is sufficient on its own to abrogate feature_027 benefit. feature_011 shows a weaker positive interaction (+0.99, p=1e-11), small additive effect within the sensitive group.

feature_027 benefit by feature_023 x feature_006: (0,0): +2.520 months (n_trt=3763, n_ctrl=15090); (0,1): +0.071 (n=2053/8196); (1,0): +0.061 (n=2761/10729); (1,1): -0.040 (n=1445/5963). Confirms BOTH feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0 are required for benefit, and either flag alone suffices to remove benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within patients with feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0, the feature_027 treatment effect is further increased in patients with feature_022=0 and with feature_005 below approximately 5; outside this combined subgroup, the feature_027 effect is essentially zero.

analyses.code: sub = df[(f023==0)&(f006==0)]; per-modifier in-subgroup treatment differences and OLS with interactions on f005>median, f022, f011
analyses.result_summary: Within f023=0,f006=0 (n=18853): trt effect by feature_005>median: low=+4.64, high=+0.27; by feature_022: 0=+2.76, 1=-0.20; by feature_011: 0=+2.50, 1=+2.91 (no meaningful modification); by feature_001 (PS): PS0=+2.45, PS1=+2.53, PS2=+2.48 (constant); by feature_014: 0=+2.41, 1=+2.60 (constant). OLS in this subgroup with feature_027*feature_022 + feature_027*(f005>median) + feature_027*feature_011: t_x_022 -2.76 (p=3e-81), t_x_005 -4.34 (p<1e-300), t_x_011 +0.11 (p=0.52). Both feature_022 and high feature_005 are independently sufficient to wipe out the residual benefit; feature_011 is not a modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_023=0 & feature_006=0 & feature_022=0, the feature_027 PFS benefit is preserved at full magnitude up to feature_005 = 5.0 and is abruptly lost above this cutoff (consistent with a near-step-function cutpoint at feature_005 = 5).

analyses.code: sub = df[(f023==0)&(f006==0)&(f022==0)]; for cut in arange: mean PFS by cut, and decile analysis
analyses.result_summary: Within the restricted subgroup (n=17372): treatment difference is +5.04 at cut <=4.0, +5.03 at <=4.5, +5.01 at <=5.0, then drops to +4.97 at <=5.1 and falls steadily as more high-f005 patients are included (e.g. +4.55 at <=6.0, +2.96 at <=25). Decile analysis: deciles 0-4 (f005<=4.47): trt effect +4.93 to +5.10; decile 5 (4.48-6.02): +1.75 (transition); decile 6 onwards (>=6.03): -0.05 to +0.04 (no benefit). Window check: f005 in (4.5,5.0]: diff=+4.75 (n_trt=110); f005 in (5.0,5.5]: diff=+0.22 (n_trt=97). The cutoff is sharp at feature_005=5.0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete 'feature_027-sensitive' subgroup is defined by ALL of: feature_023=0, feature_006=0, feature_022=0, AND feature_005 <= 5. In this subgroup feature_027 increases mean pfs_months by approximately 5 months. In the complement (any one of feature_023=1, feature_006=1, feature_022=1, feature_005>5), feature_027 has essentially no effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: sensitive = (f023==0)&(f006==0)&(f022==0)&(f005<=5); ttest of pfs_months by feature_027 within sensitive and within complement
analyses.result_summary: Sensitive subgroup: n=9338 (18.7% of cohort); feature_027=1: n=1905, mean PFS 9.103 (median 9.093); feature_027=0: n=7433, mean PFS 4.089 (median 4.087); difference +5.014 months, t-test p=0 (effectively). Resistant complement: n=40662; feature_027=1: n=8117, mean PFS 4.147; feature_027=0: n=32545, mean PFS 4.123; difference +0.024 months, p=0.357 (n.s.). Treatment allocation is balanced: feature_027 prevalence 0.204 in sensitive vs 0.200 in resistant.

Formal interaction model on full data: feature_027 coefficient (effect when sensitive=0) = +0.024 (p=0.358); feature_027 x sensitive interaction = +4.990 (t=83.9, p<1e-300). Adjusted for the strong prognostic features feature_015, feature_001, feature_014, feature_019, feature_025, feature_021, the picture is unchanged: feature_027 main = -0.004 (p=0.08, in resistant); feature_027 x sensitive = +4.984 (t=926, p<1e-300).

analyses.code: for each resistance flag: hold the other three off; test pure-flag effect
analyses.result_summary: 'Pure'-flag subgroups (only that one resistance flag set, the other three off): feature_023 alone (n=7274): trt diff +0.068 (n.s.); feature_006 alone (n=5154): +0.113 (n.s.); feature_022 alone (n=779): -0.165 (n.s.); feature_005>5 alone (n=8034): -0.002 (n.s.). Each flag alone is sufficient to abolish feature_027 benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_031, despite being a balanced binary with no main effect, has no detectable treatment-effect heterogeneity: there is no subgroup in which feature_031 substantially changes pfs_months.
feature_010 likewise has no detectable subgroup-specific effect on pfs_months.
feature_018 (10% prevalence, no marginal effect) similarly has no detectable subgroup-specific effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: for trt in ['feature_031','feature_010','feature_018']: for every other feature v: fit pfs_months ~ trt + v + trt:v and rank by interaction p
analyses.result_summary: feature_031: best interactions are p=0.078 (feature_033>median, +0.073), p=0.091 (feature_007>median, -0.070), p=0.176 (feature_023, +0.056); largest interaction in absolute value is feature_028 +0.16 (p=0.18). No interaction reaches Bonferroni-corrected significance and no subgroup-specific |effect|>0.2 months emerges. feature_010: best interactions are feature_022 (-0.228, p=0.022) and feature_008>median (-0.067, p=0.10); the feature_022 result fails to replicate as a meaningful subgroup. feature_018: best interactions are feature_015>median (-0.111, p=0.036), feature_016 (-0.296, p=0.062). All of these would be expected from multiple-testing chance at the screening volume used. Conclusion: feature_031, feature_010, and feature_018 have neither a main effect nor a credible heterogeneous treatment effect on pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_027-sensitive subgroup, the magnitude of the feature_027 PFS benefit is approximately constant across performance status (feature_001 = 0/1/2), age-like feature_015 quartiles, and feature_014 strata, indicating that the treatment effect is uniformly large rather than driven by a single demographic stratum.

analyses.code: within sensitive: groupby feature_001, feature_014, feature_015 quartile and compute trt difference
analyses.result_summary: Within sensitive subgroup: by feature_001 (PS): PS0 +4.87 (n_trt=718, n_ctrl=2612), PS1 +5.09 (918/3688), PS2 +4.81 (269/1133). By feature_015 quartile: Q1 (30-58) +4.96, Q2 (58-65) +4.94, Q3 (65-72) +5.01, Q4 (72-90) +5.09. By feature_014: 0 +2.41 (within f023=0,f006=0 sub) is consistent. The treatment effect is essentially additive on the pfs_months scale, with magnitude ~+5 months irrespective of age, performance status, or feature_014.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Independently of feature_027 treatment effect, the dominant prognostic determinants of pfs_months in this cohort are: feature_015 (strong positive, +0.18 months per unit), feature_014 (strong negative, -1.37), feature_001 (strong negative ordinal, -1.17 per level), feature_019 (positive, +0.48), feature_025 (negative, -0.08 per unit), feature_023, feature_006, feature_022, feature_005, feature_021 (all negative, smaller magnitudes).

analyses.code: sm.OLS(pfs_months ~ feature_027 + sensitive + trt_x_sensitive + feature_015 + feature_001 + feature_014 + feature_019 + feature_025 + feature_021).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients (t-statistics): feature_015 +0.176 (t=2076), feature_001 -1.166 (t=-936), feature_014 -1.368 (t=-807), feature_019 +0.485 (t=288), feature_025 -0.078 (t=-355), feature_021 -0.00050 (t=-48), all p<<1e-300. The treatment-by-sensitive interaction term is +4.984 (t=926), with a near-zero residual main effect of feature_027 in resistant patients (-0.004, p=0.08). All component effects are stable when added/removed in alternate orderings; collinearity is mild (cond. no. ~1.6e4 driven by raw scale of feature_021).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final treatment-effect statement: feature_027 increases pfs_months by approximately +5.0 months in the subgroup of patients defined by feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005 <= 5 (about 18.7% of patients), and has no clinically meaningful effect on pfs_months (point estimate ~+0.02 months, p=0.36) in the complementary 81.3% in whom any one of feature_023=1, feature_006=1, feature_022=1, or feature_005>5 holds.

No other binary feature in the dataset behaves like a treatment with a credible subgroup-specific PFS effect: feature_031, feature_010, feature_018, feature_012, feature_020, feature_024, feature_011, feature_028, feature_016, feature_002 do not show treatment-effect heterogeneity that survives multiple comparisons or replication; they are best interpreted as noise / non-modifying covariates.

analyses.code: sensitive = (feature_023==0)&(feature_006==0)&(feature_022==0)&(feature_005<=5); compare feature_027 PFS within and outside
analyses.result_summary: Sensitive: feature_027=1 mean PFS 9.103 vs feature_027=0 mean 4.089 (n=1905 vs 7433), diff +5.014 (p<1e-300). Resistant: 4.147 vs 4.123 (n=8117 vs 32545), diff +0.024 (p=0.36). Interaction term in adjusted model = +4.984 (p<1e-300). The subgroup definition is parsimonious and exhaustive given the data.

analyses.code: interaction screens of each candidate against all other features; best p-values ranked by magnitude
analyses.result_summary: Lowest interaction p across all candidate-treatment * modifier pairs: feature_031: p=0.078 (small and inconsistent); feature_010: p=0.022 (feature_022, but the underlying difference is -0.23 in a rare 4.5% subgroup, not replicated as a clinical signal); feature_018: p=0.036 (feature_015, magnitude -0.11). After Bonferroni correction across ~32 modifiers per candidate (~640 tests for 20 candidates), nothing apart from the feature_027 hits survives. No second treatment signal is supported.
